news.title: Haryana to set up panel to draft proposal for waste-to-energy projects in major cities
news.para: A committee has been formed to draft a model request for proposal for waste-to-energy projects. These projects will be undertaken in major municipal corporations like Gurugram, Faridabad, and Hisar. The initiative aims to strengthen scientific municipal solid waste management. The committee will assess feasibility, identify sites, and frame agreements.

news.title: Lucknow development body's Park View apartments to have 364 premium flats
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority is launching Park View apartments, a project featuring 364 premium 3BHK flats in Basant Kunj. Registration opens March 7th, with applications exclusively online. The complex will offer modern amenities and is expected to be completed within three years.

news.title: Amid slump, Hyderabad builders offer Harleys, EVs & gold to push home sales
news.para: Hyderabad developers are offering expensive gifts like Harley Davidsons, EVs, and gold coins to boost sluggish property sales. These incentives, ranging from Rs 3 lakh to Rs 8 lakh, aim to clear unsold inventory amid a market slowdown. While industry insiders see these as short-term sales tactics, realtors question their sustainability and impact on genuine buyer decisions.

news.title: PM inaugurates two new Delhi metro corridors
news.para: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated two new Delhi Metro corridors, the Majlis Park-Maujpur Babarpur (Pink Line) and Deepali Chowk-Majlis Park (Magenta Line), enhancing connectivity for several areas. He also laid the foundation stone for three new Phase V-A corridors, including routes to the airport and connecting Noida with south Delhi, as part of a Rs 33,500 crore infrastructure development.

news.title: Sort waste at home, get a 10% rebate on property tax: Ghaziabad civic body
news.para: Ghaziabad Municipal Corporation announced property tax rebates following Allahabad High Court's order and public protests. Residents segregating waste will receive a 10% rebate, with additional discounts for timely payments and building age. Despite these measures, some former councillors argue the revised rates remain high.

news.title: Cooperative society model not fit for managing apartments & common areas: Karnataka HC
news.para: A cooperative society is an unsuitable model for managing apartment complexes and their common areas, the High Court ruled, cancelling the registration of a Jakkur society. The court found the Karnataka Apartment Ownership Act a more appropriate framework for owner interests, citing issues with shareholding and voting restrictions under the cooperative model.

news.title: Karnataka government bans conversion for agriculture plots below five guntas
news.para: The government has stopped converting agricultural land parcels of five guntas or less for other uses. This action addresses the misuse of family partition rules. Landowners were dividing farmland into small plots for easier registration and selling them as revenue sites. The authorities aim to prevent unauthorized layouts and protect agricultural land.

news.title: Over 4,100 transit flats for Kashmiri migrant employees completed in Valley
news.para: Construction of transit flats for Kashmiri migrant employees is progressing well. Over 4,100 of the 6,000 planned flats are now complete. The remaining units are anticipated to be finished by the end of next month. Essential services are being reviewed to ensure readiness for occupancy. Further flats are scheduled for completion by April 2026.

news.title: BIS scraps new seismic zonation plan after government flags construction cost surge
news.para: India's new earthquake building safety code has been withdrawn. The Bureau of Indian Standards reversed its decision after the Cabinet Secretariat intervened. Concerns were raised about increased construction costs and insufficient consultation with stakeholders. The previous 2016 standard is now back in effect. This move impacts real estate and infrastructure projects across the country.

news.title: Maharashtra CM pushes for metro routes in newly developed areas of Pune
news.para: Pune's Metro expansion is set to receive a significant boost with the announcement of new lines under phase-II, connecting older city areas to developing regions. The Khadakwasla-Swargate-Hadapsar-Kharadi line, covering 32 km with 28 stations, is a key project expected to be executed by Maha Metro. Commuters, however, are concerned about the slow pace of construction and urge for expedited execution.

news.title: Mysuru development body plans to raise ₹1,000 crore by auctioning vacant plots online
news.para: Mysuru Development Authority aims to raise Rs 1,000 crore by auctioning 150 acres of vacant land. This initiative seeks to fund new projects, including layout development and housing, while preventing land encroachment. The auction will be conducted online, offering developed plots of various sizes to ensure better land utilization and financial stability for MDA.

news.title: West Bengal drafts plan for reuse of treated wastewater in housing units
news.para: West Bengal's urban development department has drafted a policy promoting safe reuse of treated wastewater for non-potable purposes. The policy aims to reuse at least 20% of treated wastewater by 2030 across various sectors, including industry, agriculture, and housing. It also suggests imposing user charges and commercializing treated water use for revenue generation.

news.title: About 195% more groundwater drawn by Gurugram than it replenished last year
news.para: Gurgaon's groundwater extraction is alarmingly high, exceeding permissible limits by 194.6%, a negligible improvement from last year. This over-exploitation, driven by urbanisation and industrial demand, depletes aquifers faster than they can replenish. Despite warnings, the city relies heavily on groundwater, highlighting a critical need for sustainable water management and increased rainwater harvesting.

news.title: Residents in Mohali protest over delay in PR-6 road construction
news.para: Residents protested a private builder's alleged role in delaying the PR-6 road construction in Mohali. The project, crucial for connectivity, is stalled due to a legal dispute over land compensation, impacting thousands of residents. Puda officials stated the case is sub judice and concerns public interest.

news.title: IKEA aims to be profitable by FY28; to open 25 small & medium stores in next 4-5 years
news.para: Swedish furniture giant IKEA is set to become profitable in India by FY28. The company plans to open 25 new stores across the country in the next four to five years. This expansion will focus on smaller formats to reach more customers. IKEA is also enhancing its operational efficiency and local sourcing to drive growth and improve margins.

news.title: ICICI Prudential AIF acquires Pune IT park asset for about ₹520 crore
news.para: An investment fund managed by ICICI Prudential Asset Management has bought a large office space in Pune. This acquisition is part of a strategy to build a portfolio of income-generating commercial real estate. The fund aims to provide investors access to prime office assets. This move taps into India's growing commercial real estate market.

news.title: US-based Charles Schwab leases 3.45 lakh sq ft office space in Hyderabad for GCC
news.para: Charles Schwab is expanding its global operations in India. The US financial services firm has leased a significant office space in Hyderabad's Gachibowli. This move highlights Hyderabad's growing importance as a hub for multinational companies. The lease agreement includes phased commencement and rental escalations. This expansion is expected to boost the city's office market.

news.title: DDA to take over day-to-day upkeep of flats from April
news.para: Delhi Development Authority will now manage day-to-day maintenance of its housing complexes, including pre-2021 flats, to boost sales. Flat owners will pay quarterly charges for services like security, cleaning, and minor repairs. This initiative aims to ensure efficient upkeep until occupancy reaches 80% or an RWA is registered.

news.title: Hutment sale scam returns to haunt HDIL
news.para: Mumbai Police's EOW has uncovered a Rs 150 crore housing scam involving HDIL Group, where possession documents for 1,000 slum rehab residents were allegedly sold to third parties. Former staffer's statement points to HDIL promoters, the Wadhwans, selling these files for substantial sums, leading to unauthorized occupants in prime transit housing.
